---
layout: post
title: "The Folder Nobody Locked"
subtitle: "HTB Enigma, where a world-readable share hands you a new hire's key, one password wears three faces, an invoice filename speaks to a shell, and a safe command runner turns out to run anything at all"
date: 2026-07-12 12:00:00 +0000
description: "HTB Enigma walkthrough. An easy Linux box that chains a world-readable NFS leak and password reuse into a blind, egress-filtered RCE and a root-owned command runner. Sealed while the box is live."
image: /assets/og/enigma.png
tags: [hackthebox, writeup]
---

Enigma opens with more doors than most, and one of them is not a door at all, just a hallway with the lights left on. A world-readable share hands you a new hire's onboarding packet, and the password inside is loyal to nobody, opening mail for two people and, later, the machine itself. From there the box turns quiet on you. You get code execution early and it does you almost no good, because the firewall has cut the outbound line and every error is swallowed whole. The whole middle of this box is learning to hear a machine that refuses to speak, by making it leave notes in a room you already hold a key to. The end is a tool built to make commands safe, running as root, running anything you like.

```
        e n i g m a            (easy · linux)
        =========================
        a folder left on the curb.   you read the key.
        one password, three faces.   every lock opens.
        the box goes silent.         you make it write.
                        |
                        v
        an invoice filename speaks to a shell,
        and a safe command runner runs anything.
                        |
                        v
        every lock here is somebody trusting a label,
        a shared folder, or a reused password to be
        exactly the harmless thing it claimed to be.
                                              . . .
```

The full chain, from the folder on the curb to root, is written up below in the usual detail. Because Enigma is **still active on Hack The Box**, that write-up is sealed. It unseals the moment the box retires. If you already hold the passphrase, the gate is right here.

<script type="application/json" id="lock-data">{"v":1,"iter":250000,"salt":"pMt3+EFMU/9d15E+sNZnhQ==","iv":"EtXcDZJd0EH9mhYc","ct":""}</script>

{% include locked-writeup.html %}

---

*HTB: Enigma, an easy Linux box that is really a lecture on shared folders, reused passwords, and the difference between a label and an order. Still active. The full chain unseals here the day it retires, no live payloads either way.*
